reference.title: Using IPsec on a system with FIPS mode enabled

section.p: RHEL in Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) mode exclusively uses validated cryptographic modules, automatically disabling legacy protocols and ciphers. Enabling FIPS mode is often a requirement for federal compliance and enhances system security.

The Libreswan IPsec implementation provided by RHEL is fully FIPS-compliant. When the system is in FIPS mode, Libreswan automatically uses the certified cryptographic modules without requiring any additional configuration, regardless of whether Libreswan is installed on a new FIPS-enabled system or when FIPS mode is activated on a system with an existing Libreswan VPN.
If FIPS mode is enabled, you can confirm that Libreswan is running in FIPS mode:
To list the allowed algorithms and ciphers in Libreswan in FIPS mode, enter:
section.codeblock: # ipsec pluto --selftest 2>&1 ... 000 FIPS Encryption algorithms: 000 AES_CCM_16 {256,192,*128} IKEv1: ESP IKEv2: ESP FIPS aes_ccm, aes_ccm_c 000 AES_CCM_12 {256,192,*128} IKEv1: ESP IKEv2: ESP FIPS aes_ccm_b 000 AES_CCM_8 {256,192,*128} IKEv1: ESP IKEv2: ESP FIPS aes_ccm_a 000 ...
